Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Carmen Reyes / UrbanNest using search_emails (query: "Carmen" or "UrbanNest" or "housing" or "Mehta"). Find the trigger email from Carmen (April 26, 7:45 AM) reporting that all three holds expired, Option B (Streeterville, The Pinnacle) is gone (claimed by Deloitte), Options A and C still available for 24 hours.
Search emails for Dr. Mehta's response using search_emails (query: "Mehta" or "housing preference" or "Streeterville" or "Pinnacle"). Discover Dr. Mehta's email to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io (April 25, 4:30 PM) where he selected Option B (Streeterville). He replied to Patricia at Canopy instead of to Fatimah, which is why Fatimah never received the selection. He also mentions his lab director Dr. Fiona Walsh needs 48-hour advance notice for the equipment chain-of-custody handoff.
Search Slack for messages about Mehta housing preference using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mehta" or "Streeterville" or "Option B" or "housing preference"). Find Mina Hashimoto's message in #operations (C006, April 25, 5:45 PM) flagging that Patricia forwarded Dr. Mehta's preference for Option B and noting she wasn't sure if it reached Fatimah. Mina is offline for the weekend.
Search Linear for Dr. Mehta tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta" or "Canopy Health"). Find three tickets: #343 (Temporary Housing, due May 1, in progress), #375 (Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody, due April 30, in progress), #396 (Household Move Boston to Chicago, due May 3, in progress). All assigned to Fatimah.
Read Linear comments on housing ticket #343 using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_bd446671ceed). Find the comment with the three housing options forwarded to Dr. Mehta, recommending Option B (Streeterville), deadline April 25.
Read Linear comments on equipment ticket #375 using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a). Find the comment with confirmed equipment inventory: 2x Eizo RadiForce RX660 monitors (S/N RX660-1147A, RX660-1203A), SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet (~350 lbs), chain-of-custody requirements.
Read Linear comments on equipment ticket #375 using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a). In addition to the existing equipment inventory comment, find Chloe Vance's comment (April 25, 3:30 PM) flagging a scheduling conflict from Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations: the specialty electronics crew has a conflict on April 28, alternatives are April 27 (recommended, needs confirmation tonight, delivery April 29) or May 1 (delivery May 3, zero buffer for May 5 deadline). Chloe also notes the DOT documentation surcharge of $175 and tags Fatimah to coordinate with Dr. Mehta's lab director for the chain-of-custody handoff.
Search Airtable for Dr. Mehta relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblRelocations01, query: "Mehta" or "Anand"). No record exists — this is a tracking gap that needs to be fixed.
Search CRM for Canopy Health / Mehta deal using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Canopy" or company_id: "company_canopy"). Find deal crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb: $12,170, stage "inprogress", linked to company_canopy. Note the estimate EST-2026-0422 in QuickBooks.
Look up Carmen Reyes's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Carmen" or "Reyes"). Find carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com.
Send email to Carmen Reyes using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) to re-secure housing. Since Dr. Mehta's preference was Option B (Streeterville) which is gone, and the proximity to Northwestern/Prentice was the deciding factor (for Priya's prenatal care), Option A (River North, $1,250/week, 5 min to Northwestern) is the best remaining alternative. Email should confirm selection of Option A (or C if reasoning differently), request immediate re-hold, and mention the May 2 move-in target.
Update Linear ticket #343 (housing) using linear_create_comment and/or linear_update_issue (issueId: linear_issue_bd446671ceed) with status update: Option B lost, Dr. Mehta had selected it but response went to Canopy directly, re-securing Option A (or C), new hold confirmed.
Update Linear ticket #375 (equipment) using linear_create_comment and/or linear_update_issue (issueId: linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a) with status update: Swift has scheduling conflict on April 28, Greg Pallone offered April 27 or May 1 alternatives, lab director Dr. Fiona Walsh needs 48-hour notice for chain-of-custody handoff.
Update Linear ticket #396 (household move) using linear_create_comment (issueId: linear_issue_4902f5c68acb) with status update noting downstream impact from the housing and equipment complications: temporary housing situation changed (Option B lost, re-securing alternative), equipment pickup date may shift from April 28 to April 27 or May 1 depending on Swift confirmation, overall move timeline still targeting May 5 on-site date.
Create Airtable record for Dr. Mehta using airtable_create_record (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblRelocations01, fields: Name: Dr. Anand Mehta, Company: Canopy Health, Status: In Progress, Origin: Boston MA, Destination: Chicago IL, Assigned Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Special Requirements: Executive white-glove move, 2x DICOM-calibrated medical monitors + secured filing cabinet with chain-of-custody, partner 7 months pregnant).
Log investigation findings as an engagement note on the Canopy deal using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: housing hold expired overnight, Option B (Streeterville) lost to Deloitte, Dr. Mehta had selected it via email to Patricia Langford but response never reached MoveOps, re-securing Option A (River North), Swift equipment pickup conflict on April 28 being rescheduled, company_ids: ["company_canopy"]).
Look up Mina Hashimoto's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina"). Find mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
Send email to Mina Hashimoto using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) with consolidated status update: housing hold expired, Option B gone, Dr. Mehta had selected it but responded to Patricia instead of MoveOps, re-securing alternative option, Swift has equipment pickup schedule conflict, all workstream statuses with current blockers.
Look up Patricia Langford's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia" or "Langford"). Find patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io.
Send email to Patricia Langford using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io) explaining: we received Dr. Mehta's preference for Streeterville (Option B) but unfortunately the hold expired and the unit was claimed, we are securing the best available alternative (Option A or C), and requesting she relay to Dr. Mehta with apologies. Also mention the equipment pickup scheduling needs (48-hour advance notice for Dr. Fiona Walsh).
Post in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006) alerting the team about the housing situation, confirming awareness of the Swift scheduling conflict from Chloe's Linear comment, and summarizing current status of all three workstreams with blockers.
Look up Greg Pallone's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg" or "Pallone"). Find greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com.
Send email to Greg Pallone using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) confirming the preferred equipment pickup date (April 27 or May 1, depending on agent's reasoning about the 48-hour notice requirement from Dr. Mehta's lab director). Also coordinate with Dr. Mehta or Patricia about notifying the lab director Dr. Fiona Walsh for the chain-of-custody handoff.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

2 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) to [carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com](mailto:carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) requests a re-hold or confirmation for one of the remaining available options: Option A (River North, The Langham Residences) or Option C (Gold Coast, 1030 N State St)
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly requests re-hold on Option A (River North, The Langham Residences, 750 N Clark St) and Option C (Gold Coast, The Elysian, 1030 N State St), not Option B. Option A is flagged as primary selection and Option C as backup.
✓Run #2The email to Carmen explicitly requests 'immediate holds on BOTH' Option A (River North, The Langham Residences) and Option C (Gold Coast, The Elysian). Option B is not requested. Both remaining valid options are covered.
✓Run #3The email body to Carmen explicitly requests to 'lock in Option A — The Langham Residences, 750 N Clark St, Unit 2207, River North, $1,250/week — effective immediately,' with Option C (Gold Coast) as fallback. Option B (Streeterville) is not requested.
✓Run #4The email to Carmen explicitly requests: 'Please re-hold BOTH Option A (River North, The Langham Residences, 750 N Clark St, Unit 2207) and Option C (Gold Coast, The Elysian, 1030 N State St, Unit 1806) for 24 hours.' Option B (Streeterville) is not requested.
✓Run #5The email body to Carmen explicitly requests Option A (River North, The Langham Residences, 750 N Clark St, Unit 2207, $1,250/week) and lists Option C (Gold Coast, 1030 N State St) as backup. Option B (Streeterville) is not requested.
✓Run #6The email to Carmen explicitly mentions Option A (River North, The Langham Residences, 750 N Clark St, Unit 2207, $1,250/week) and Option C (Gold Coast, The Elysian, 1030 N State St, Unit 1806, $1,450/week) and requests immediate holds on both. Option B (Streeterville) is correctly described as gone and not requested.
3 The housing Linear issue (issueId: linear_issue_bd446671ceed, ticket #343) was updated (via linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue) and the tool returned success.

4 The Linear update on the housing ticket (#343), whether via linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue, mentions that Option B (Streeterville or The Pinnacle) is no longer available and an alternative option is being secured.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Linear update on ticket #343 explicitly states 'Option B claimed by Deloitte at 6:15 AM Saturday April 26' and notes 'RECOMMENDED: Option A (River North, Langham Residences)... BACKUP: Option C (Gold Coast)' as alternatives being pursued.

9 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) to [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com](mailto:mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) explains that Dr. Mehta responded to Patricia Langford at Canopy instead of to Fatimah at MoveOps, which is why the housing preference was never received.
outcome

✓Run #3The email sent to both Patricia and Mina clearly explains the root cause: 'Dr. Mehta made his selection...but he sent his response to you, Patricia, rather than directly to me. By the time this was flagged on our side Friday evening, the holds had expired.' This directly explains the misdirected email.
✓Run #4The email to Mina explicitly states: 'Dr. Mehta replied to Patricia on Friday choosing Option B (Streeterville), but the reply went to Patricia instead of me.' This clearly explains the root cause — the housing preference went to the wrong person.
✓Run #5The email body to Mina explicitly explains: 'Dr. Mehta communicated his housing preference (Option B, Streeterville, The Pinnacle) to Patricia on Thursday, and Mina flagged this on Slack Friday evening. Unfortunately, I did not see either message before UrbanNest's 48-hour holds expired.' This clearly establishes that Mehta replied to Patricia instead of Fatimah.
✓Run #6The email sent to Patricia (CC'd to Mina) explicitly states: 'Dr. Mehta sent his housing selection (Option B, the Streeterville unit at The Pinnacle) to you on Friday rather than to me directly. Unfortunately, by the time that got to our side, the 48-hour holds on all three units had expired.' This explains the misdirected email as root cause.
